reasoning: Tajima Beef (reg#2) and Kobe Beef (reg#3) appear correctly at the top with accurate GI descriptions and MAFF source URLs. However, the query asks specifically about Tajima beef as Kobe's origin stock; the response continues into unrelated Hyogo GI products (soybean, fugu) without any signal about how to narrow. An agent would need to filter manually.
missing_data: No explicit explanation of the Tajima→Kobe lineage hierarchy in the structured data; no URL or contact for Tajima beef producers separate from the Kobe association.
improvement_hint: Add a keyword-filter pass so a query for '但馬牛' returns that record first, and surface the Kobe→Tajima parent-child relationship as a structured field rather than burying it in free-text descriptions.

reasoning: Same data as L1-01 but in Arabic. Tajima Beef and Kobe Beef entries are correctly translated and appear first. Arabic descriptions are accurate and fluent. Same coverage-dilution issue: the response includes unrelated Hyogo GI items. The transliteration of non-food product names (e.g. 'ساو موتشي دايزو') is phonetic-only, which is acceptable for an MCP data layer.
missing_data: No Arabic-language producer contact or visit information for Tajima region.
improvement_hint: Surface keyword relevance score so Arabic-speaking agents can rank the Tajima/Kobe entries and discard unrelated items without manual filtering.

reasoning: The query explicitly asks about Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields, not Uji, not Kyoto city. The tool returned get_japan_heritage for Kyoto prefecture, which surfaces Nijojo, Toji, Nishi Honganji—all Kyoto city landmarks with no relevance to Minami-Yamashiro's tea terraces. The constraint 'just the village, not Uji' was ignored entirely.
missing_data: Any Japan Heritage story or wikidata entry for Minami-Yamashiro village; the Wazuka/Minami-Yamashiro tea landscape data.
improvement_hint: Route village-specific queries to search_area or get_spots with municipality filter; get_japan_heritage at prefecture level is too coarse for sub-municipal intents.

reasoning: Yoshida no Hi-Matsuri appears correctly at top as 重要無形民俗文化財, located in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi. Spanish translation is accurate. The second local item is an unrelated Yamanashi fireworks event without Spanish translation. national_heritage section lists UNESCO ICH items from other prefectures, which dilutes focus. Date field is null for the main item.
missing_data: Date of Yoshida Fire Festival (August 26-27 annually); exact location at Kitaguchi Hongu Fuji Sengen Shrine; description beyond the bare minimum.
improvement_hint: Populate date field for bunka_intangible records from Wikidata P585/P580; filter national_heritage to items geographically near the query prefecture by default.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Nakagi-region yuzu (中芸ゆず) in Kochi. The response returns 500 of 685 Kochi food items with no keyword filtering applied. The top result is 物部ゆず (a different yuzu GI from Kami City), and 中芸ゆず does not appear in the visible portion. The scraped_local_food item is a generic Kochi tourism portal page, not food data. Constraint (specific sub-region product) was not respected.
missing_data: 中芸ゆず GI record (if registered); any structured data on yuzu cultivation in Nahari/Kitagawa/Tano/Yasuda/Umaji municipalities.
wrong_data: scraped_local_food item '観光スポット検索' is not a food item at all—it is a tourism spot page leaking into the food results.
improvement_hint: Apply keyword filter when q is implicitly present in the intent; add category guard to exclude scraped_local_food items that lack a food-category tag.

reasoning: get_spots for Hirosaki returns Hirosaki Castle (must_see, sakura_meisho_100) and Hirosaki Park (must_see, sakura_meisho_100, garden) at the top, correctly covering the cherry blossom intent. Chinese query receives Chinese names. Heritage designations are present. Municipality field shows '青森県' instead of '弘前市' for some entries, which is a minor data quality gap.
missing_data: Bloom season dates (late April); no description of the castle moat cherry blossom spectacle that is the main draw.
wrong_data: municipality field '青森県' instead of '弘前市' for Hirosaki Castle entry is imprecise.
improvement_hint: Populate municipality_name correctly at city level for spot entries; add seasonal_highlight field where sakura_meisho_100 designation is present.

reasoning: search_area for 出雲大社 returns the main shrine (Q696362) first with full metadata: coordinates, website, opening hours, National Treasure designation, prominence_score 0.9. The Honden (main building) and the station appear next. Response correctly returns Korean-language name where available. The station entry has kinds=['shinto_shrine'] which is semantically wrong, but this is a minor data issue.
missing_data: No Korean description text for the shrine itself; no mention of the October Kamiari festival significance.
wrong_data: Izumo Taisha-mae Station entry has kinds=['shinto_shrine']—a station is not a shrine.
improvement_hint: Audit kinds tags for non-shrine entities that inherit shrine classification by proximity; add a description_ko field from Wikidata labels.

reasoning: search_area for 厳島 returns Itsukushima island (prominence 1.0) first, then Itsukushima Shrine (prominence 0.95) with UNESCO WHS and National Treasure designations. The Otorii gate and West Corridor follow. Query was French but tool called with lang=en; agent will need to translate, but the data itself is complete and accurate.
missing_data: No ferry access information from Miyajima-guchi; tide timing for the famous floating torii effect.
improvement_hint: When lang≠en, include the requested language name field (name_fr) in search_area results if translation data exists; add access_note for island attractions requiring ferry.

reasoning: The tool is get_japan_heritage for Wakayama, which correctly surfaces Kumano Kodo at position 3 with UNESCO WHS designation and full description. However, the tool_scope_note explicitly warns that Kumano Kodo should use search_area or get_traditional_arts—yet the top results are Koyasan temples (not the pilgrimage route). An agent following the scope note correctly would call a second tool; the Kumano Kodo entry does appear but is buried.
missing_data: Route-specific information (Nakahechi, Kohechi sub-routes); recommended starting points; seasonal conditions.
improvement_hint: When a canonical wikidata_heritage entry with kinds=['kumano_kodo','pilgrimage_route'] exists, boost it to position 1 regardless of prominence_score in get_japan_heritage results.

reasoning: search_area for 姫路城 returns the main castle first (prominence 1.0) with UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, Special Historic Site, opening hours, phone, wheelchair info, and fee. Multiple sub-structures follow. Query was Russian but data is complete and usable in English. The tool returns 66 matches; the top entries are all directly relevant to Himeji Castle complex.
missing_data: No Russian description text; no mention of the White Heron nickname or the 2009-2015 renovation context.
improvement_hint: Add a brief description_en field pulled from Wikidata sitelinks for the main castle entry to give the agent narrative context beyond the structural metadata.

reasoning: get_traditional_arts with lang=th correctly returns Bunraku (UNESCO ICH 2003) first in Thai. However, query asks specifically about Osaka performances; no prefecture or location filter was applied. The tool returns 183 items nationally with no Osaka-specific filtering. Items 3+ are unrelated to Bunraku or Osaka. An agent would need to know to call get_spots(prefecture=Osaka) for the National Bunraku Theatre.
missing_data: National Bunraku Theatre (国立文楽劇場) Osaka address and performance schedule; Osaka as the primary performance venue.
improvement_hint: Add a prefecture_filter or location parameter to get_traditional_arts; or include a see_also_venues section linking UNESCO ICH items to their primary institutional performance venues.

reasoning: Query asks about Yumihama Kasuri (弓浜絣), a METI traditional craft from Tottori. get_local_specialty for Tottori returns 7 items; all are MAFF GI food products (rakkyo, broccoli, persimmon, watermelon). Yumihama Kasuri is absent. The tool should have been called with category=craft, or get_traditional_arts should have been used. Core retrieval failure.
missing_data: Yumihama Kasuri METI designation record; indigo-dyed cotton kasuri weaving description; production area (Sakaiminato, Yonago area).
improvement_hint: When intent clearly targets a craft (kasuri, weave, textile), route to get_local_specialty(category='craft') or get_traditional_arts automatically; add Yumihama Kasuri to METI data if not already indexed.

reasoning: search_area for 直島 returns Benesse Art Site Naoshima and the new museum at top, followed by Naoshima Town municipality and multiple scraped tourism pages. Core art island intent is addressed. Scraped spots are in Chinese Traditional (from a zh_tw URL), creating language inconsistency in an English response. Chichu Art Museum not explicitly named separately.
missing_data: Chichu Art Museum (地中美術館) as a distinct named entity; Yayoi Kusama pumpkin sculpture reference; ferry access from Uno port/Takamatsu.
improvement_hint: Deduplicate scraped spots that link to the same domain (naoshima.net zh_tw and ja versions create redundant entries); ensure language-matched spot names appear first.

reasoning: get_spots for Kakunodate returns the samurai district (prominence 1.15) with full heritage designations (IPDT, Place of Scenic Beauty), weeping cherry trees as a Natural Monument, and the Cherry Bark Work Museum. The description_en for the main entry is rich and accurate. All key elements of a samurai district query are covered. Municipality correctly shows 仙北市.
missing_data: Specific open samurai residences (Ishiguro-ke, Aoyagi-ke) as named child entities; spring sakura festival dates.
improvement_hint: Add child-entity links for individual yashiki (residences) within the preservation district so agents can enumerate specific visitable buildings.

reasoning: get_spots for Onomichi returns Shimanami Kaido at prominence 1.0 first, which is a cycling expressway connecting Onomichi to Shikoku—technically correct but broader than the town itself. The Onomichi City Museum and Onomichi Castle follow. The town's famous temple walk (千光寺, rope way, cat alley) is not visible in the top results. Scraped spots appear at 'broader' tier without descriptions.
missing_data: Senkoji Temple and ropeway; Cat Alley (猫の細道); the famous hill-temple walking path that defines Onomichi tourism.
improvement_hint: Boost spots with municipality_code matching 34205 (Onomichi-shi) and add pilgrimage/temple-walk kind tags; the Shimanami Kaido entry is geographically broader and should be tier='broader' not the lead result.

reasoning: search_area for 那智 returns Nachi Falls (prominence 0.95) first with correct Wakayama location, Natural Monument and Place of Scenic Beauty designations, coordinates, and visit duration. The 48 Waterfalls follow, then Nachi Primeval Forest. One anomalous entry (那智滝図 painting in Tokyo) appears in the results but is clearly labeled Tokyo, so an agent can discard it. Coverage is excellent.
missing_data: No mention of Kumano Nachi Taisha shrine adjacent to the falls; no entry fee information for the falls viewing platform.
improvement_hint: Add a filter to suppress results where prefecture_code differs from the queried region when a geographically-specific query is made; link the shrine and falls as co-located entities.

reasoning: search_area for 知床 returns the generic entity, peninsula, and national park all with UNESCO WHS designation and Hokkaido location. The World Heritage Conservation Center follows with website and access. Query was Hindi but data is complete in English. Coverage is strong with 90 matches. No hallucinations.
missing_data: Specific recommended activities (Shiretoko Five Lakes, Kamuiwakka hot spring falls); wildlife (brown bear, Steller sea eagle) viewing seasons.
improvement_hint: Add activity_tags or seasonal_highlight fields for national park entries so agents can surface experiential recommendations without requiring additional tool calls.

reasoning: search_area for 出羽 returns Dewa Sanzan (prominence 0.9) first with Japan Heritage designation and sacred_mountain kinds. The Dewa Sanzan Shrine entry appears at position 5. However, other 出羽 results (Tebajima island in Tokushima, Dewazakura Museum, Dewa Mountains in Aomori) dilute the response. The query is specifically about Dewa Sanzan in Yamagata, but the broad kanji match returns off-topic results.
missing_data: Individual mountain names (Haguro, Gas-san, Yudono-san); yamabushi ascetic pilgrimage context; seasonal closure of Gas-san and Yudono-san.
improvement_hint: Use prefecture_code constraint when disambiguation is possible; the 出羽 search returns Tokushima and Aomori results that an agent must filter manually, increasing error risk.

reasoning: search_area for 屋久島 returns the island first (prominence 1.0) with UNESCO WHS, Ramsar, and Wilderness Area designations. National park, World Heritage Center, History Museum, and local shrine follow. Portuguese query receives English data—sufficient for agent use. The heritage_designations array is rich and accurate.
missing_data: Jomon Sugi cedar trail access and permit details; recommended hiking seasons (October–November, March–May); Shiratani Unsuikyo rainforest.
improvement_hint: Add trekking/hiking kind tags and a permits_required boolean to national park and UNESCO WHS island entries to inform agents of access constraints.

reasoning: Query asks about Sado Island broadly; tool used is get_japan_heritage for Niigata. Sado Gold Mine correctly appears in top_canonical_heritage with UNESCO WHS designation (confirmed 2024). The body of the Japan Heritage items is about Shinano River flame-shaped pottery, not Sado Island at all. Sado Island as a geographical and cultural entity is not directly addressed beyond the mines.
missing_data: Sado Island as an entity (Q11332823 or similar); Toki (Japanese crested ibis) conservation; traditional Kodo drumming; oni-daiko and noh theater traditions.
improvement_hint: When top_canonical_heritage matches the intent, surface a see_also_spots section linking to search_area results for the same entity; Sado Island deserves a direct get_spots call alongside the heritage data.

reasoning: Query asks for 'Tohoku region' traditional onsen ryokan. Tool called with prefecture=Akita only. Akita is one of six Tohoku prefectures. The response returns valid 秘湯 ryokan in Akita (Taka-no-yu, Abe Ryokan, Okuyama Ryokan, etc.) but misses Yamagata, Iwate, Aomori, Miyagi, Fukushima onsen. Coordinates are null for all entries, limiting practical use. Tohoku multi-prefecture constraint was ignored.
missing_data: Onsen ryokan in Yamagata (Ginzan Onsen), Iwate (Tsunagi Onsen, Hanamaki), Aomori (Sukayu), Miyagi (Naruko), Fukushima (Higashiyama Onsen).
improvement_hint: Support a region parameter in get_hotels that maps to multiple prefectures; alternatively, auto-expand to all Tohoku prefecture codes when 東北 region is detected in intent.

reasoning: Query asks for shukubo (temple lodgings) on the Shikoku Henro pilgrimage. Tool returns generic Tokushima hotels from OSM: a guest house, an onsen hotel, and regular ryokan. None are shukubo. The Shikoku Henro route spans all four Shikoku prefectures, but even within Tokushima the response contains no temple lodgings. Shukubo is a specific accommodation type not indexed in the hotel data.
missing_data: Any of the ~88 Shikoku henro temples offering shukubo; Ryozenji (Temple 1) and others with registered shukubo. Pilgrimage-specific lodging type entirely absent.
improvement_hint: Add a shukubo lodging_type tag for temple-run accommodations; ingest the 88-temple shukubo registry as a dedicated data source; surface henro-route hotels with ohenro-san welcome flags from OSM data.

reasoning: Query asks for Hokkaido farm experience (農家体験/agri-tourism). get_spots for Hokkaido returns volcano Showa-shinzan, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle, and Daisetsuzan—all landmarks with zero relevance to farm stays or agricultural experiences. The tool has no farm-stay or agri-tourism data type. Complete retrieval failure for this intent type.
missing_data: Any agri-tourism or farm-stay (農家民宿) data for Hokkaido; lavender farms (富良野); dairy/cheese experience farms; potato/wheat harvest programs.
improvement_hint: Add agri_tourism and farm_stay as lodging/activity types; scrape MAFF Green Tourism registry or Hokkaido's official agri-tourism portal to fill this coverage gap.

reasoning: Query asks where to see pottery made in Kyushu. get_local_specialty(Saga, craft) returns Imari-Arita Ware and Karatsu Ware with METI designations, production area text (specific cities), and accurate English descriptions. The Hakata Ori (weaving) entry is tangential but technically correct for Saga. The see_also section adds a museum with opening hours. Addresses and kiln-tour information are absent but the craft designations give agent clear direction.
missing_data: Specific kiln studios (窯元) open for visits in Arita/Imari/Karatsu; no mention of Nabeshima porcelain as an adjacent designation; Kyushu-wide coverage missing (Karatsu covers part but not Satsuma or Hagi).
improvement_hint: Add a kiln_tours_available boolean or link to official workshop visitor pages for METI craft entries; extend query to cover Fukuoka/Nagasaki/Kagoshima prefectures for broader Kyushu pottery coverage.

reasoning: Query asks for Tohoku traditional festivals. Tool called with prefecture=Aomori only. Aomori Nebuta and Hachinohe Sansha Taisai appear correctly. But Tohoku has six prefectures; Sendai Tanabata (Miyagi), Akita Kanto, Yamagata Hanagasa, Morioka Sansa Odori are all missing. national_heritage section lists non-Tohoku UNESCO ICH items (Kawagoe-Saitama, Uozu-Toyama) which dilute the regional focus.
missing_data: Festivals from Miyagi, Akita, Yamagata, Iwate, Fukushima; Sendai Tanabata, Akita Kanto Matsuri, Yamagata Hanagasa are flagship Tohoku festivals entirely absent.
improvement_hint: Implement a Tohoku region shortcut that queries all six prefectures; filter national_heritage to geographically adjacent prefectures rather than returning all Japan-wide UNESCO ICH.
